overview: The design contains a single independent Go service with no dependencies, so all implementation happens in a single foundational phase.

phases.name: Foundation Service
phases.description: Build the standalone todo-api service which has no dependencies.

components.name: todo-api
responsibilities: Expose versioned /v1 REST endpoints for CRUD operations on todo items
Validate input and return structured JSON error responses with appropriate HTTP status codes
Persist todos in a relational database with versioned migrations applied at startup
Provide health and readiness probes, request logging, and graceful shutdown
Support pagination, filtering, sorting, and optimistic concurrency on updates
components.architectureContext: Single self-contained backend service that fronts a PostgreSQL (or SQLite fallback) database. It is called directly by API clients and has no upstream service dependencies within the system.
keyConsiderations: Use embed.FS for SQL migrations executed on startup; ensure idempotency
Implement optimistic concurrency via version column — distinguish 404 vs 409 on update
Enforce http.MaxBytesReader and content-type checks; map malformed JSON to 400 with structured error
Wrap DB errors to return 503 on connectivity failures rather than crashing
Index columns used in filters/sort (completed, priority, due_date, created_at) to meet <200ms list SLA on 100k rows
